title: Domain finding intake (pool ingest + dedup)
description: Provide the seam domains write AUTO-surfaced findings through: an exported ingestFinding(store, finding, {now}) and a `bober task ingest` CLI that reads a Finding JSON (file or stdin). Ingest assigns/uses a content-derived stable id (hash of domain+title+kind) so a re-surfaced finding reconciles to NOOP/UPDATE rather than duplicating, validates against the Finding schema, and the finding then appears in `bober task list`.

successCriteria.description: `npm run build` exits 0 with ingestFinding and the `bober task ingest` subcommand added.
A unit test ingests a watch-kind Finding JSON and asserts it appears in the active hub pool and is returned by the list query.
A unit test ingests two findings with identical domain+title+kind and asserts exactly one active row exists for that id afterward (dedup via reconcile NOOP/UPDATE, not a second ADD).
A unit test feeds malformed JSON / a payload missing required Finding fields and asserts ingest rejects it, the CLI sets exitCode=1, and no row is written and nothing is thrown.
`npm run typecheck` exits 0 — ingestFinding accepts the imported Finding type and returns a typed reconcile action.

nonGoals: Do NOT implement any domain's proactive review pass that generates findings — only the ingest entry point they call.
Do NOT bypass the Finding schema — every ingested payload is validated before persistence.
Do NOT duplicate captureTask's persistence — reuse writeFinding/writeFact.
Do NOT read the clock inside ingestFinding — now is injected.
stopConditions: `npm run build` and `npm run typecheck` exit 0 and the ingest + dedup + rejection unit tests pass.
Ingesting a sample domain Finding makes it visible in `bober task list`, and ingesting it again leaves a single active row.
definitionOfDone: Domain pipelines have one entry point — ingestFinding(store, finding, {now}) plus `bober task ingest` (reading a Finding JSON from a file path or stdin) — to push an AUTO-surfaced finding into the unified hub pool. Ingest derives a stable id from domain+title+kind so a repeated finding reconciles to a single active row instead of duplicating, validates the payload against the imported Finding schema, surfaces the finding in `bober task list`, and reports malformed input on stderr with exitCode=1 without throwing.
assumptions: Stable id = a short hash of `${domain}|${title}|${kind}` (mirroring factId in src/state/facts.ts:58-69) so re-surfacing collides on subject and reconciles (reconcile.ts:56-78).
ingestFinding reuses writeFinding (which routes through writeFact) so the reconcile NOOP/UPDATE/ADD logic applies for free.
A finding supplied without an id gets the derived id; a finding with an id keeps it.
generatorNotes: Add ingestFinding(store, finding, {now}) to src/hub/finding-store.ts: validate finding via the imported Finding schema (.parse — throws into a caught boundary), derive id when absent as a 16-char hash of `${domain}|${title}|${kind}` (reuse the createHash('sha256').slice(0,16) approach from factId at src/state/facts.ts:58-69), set surfacedAt=now if absent, then call writeFinding (so reconcile dedups). Return the ReconcileAction. Add a `ingest` subcommand to src/cli/commands/task.ts taking an optional <file> arg (read stdin when omitted), JSON.parse the payload, call ingestFinding, and print the action (added/updated/unchanged). On parse/validation failure: chalk.red message, exitCode=1, return — write nothing. Keep now injected at the boundary.
evaluatorNotes: Verify dedup: ingest the same domain+title+kind twice with an in-memory store and assert getActiveFacts('hub',undefined,'finding') has length 1. Verify validation rejects a payload missing required schema fields and that NO row is written on rejection. Confirm ingestFinding reuses writeFinding/writeFact rather than a raw insertFact. Confirm the derived id is deterministic (same inputs -> same id) and that now is a parameter.
